text:
**Bottom line recommendation.**  
deny_broad_change_as_submitted remains the only posture supported by present facts. In parallel, continue manual workaround while scoped controls are prepared, require business_owner_security_and_incident_commander approval before release, and require rollback_command_owner_verification_and_backup exception review. These three actions must run first. Only after they close the documented gaps may conditionally_approve_narrow_time_boxed_change or stage_canary_replay_then_expand_only_if_metrics_pass be considered. No evidence currently justifies executing the broad EC-9217 change.

**What is happening and why it matters now.**  
PortBridge Systems is 3.5 hours into a Severity 1 ingestion outage. A contractual customer window closes in 90 minutes. The proposed EC-9217 opens the security group to 0.0.0.0/0 for 24 hours, grants bucket-wide IAM read/write/delete, and lowers storage controls. SOURCE_PACKET_MD records incident-commander approval only; business-owner and security approvals are absent. Object-level logging is disabled on one bucket; monitoring does not cover prefix bleed or unexpected deletes. The last metadata snapshot is 19 hours old against a 4-hour RPO. These are direct packet facts, not inferences.

**Strongest evidence.**  
S9_DERIVED_D10_PRODUCTION_CHANGE_BOUNDARY_TABLE quantifies customer exposure at $48,000 per hour, producing $168,000 gross exposure already and $72,000 more if the window is missed. The manual workaround sustains 45 percent throughput. A 55-minute staging rollback test plus policy/access preview fits inside the remaining window if started immediately. These arithmetic statements rest solely on the frozen packet.

**Weak, stale, or conflicting evidence.**  
S2_NIST_SP800_128_CONFIGURATION_MANAGEMENT_STALE and S7_NIST_SP800_61_R2_INCIDENT_HANDLING_WITHDRAWN are withdrawn and supply no current authority. S8_WIKIPEDIA_ITSM_CHANGE_MANAGEMENT_LIMITED_CONTEXT is a weak encyclopedia entry. S1_NIST_SP800_53_R5_CONFIGURATION_AUDIT_CONTROLS, S3_AWS_IAM_SECURITY_BEST_PRACTICES, S4_NIST_SP800_207_ZERO_TRUST_ARCHITECTURE, and S6_CIS_CONTROLS_V8_1_LOGGING_CHANGE_ACCESS emphasize least-privilege and formal change control but do not certify that 0.0.0.0/0 or bucket-wide delete is required. S5_NIST_SP800_34_R1_CONTINGENCY_PLANNING supplies the strongest continuity pressure yet states explicitly that it “does not excuse missing rollback, backup freshness, logging, approval, or least-privilege gates.” No packet evidence shows why the narrower /29-plus-prefix alternative was rejected.

**Calculations or data interpretation.**  
The 19-hour snapshot lies 15 hours outside the 4-hour RPO. The requested 24-hour broad opening is 16 times the 90-minute customer window by duration. The 55-minute test leaves 35 minutes of slack only if the first attempt succeeds; the prior EC-9217 attempt already failed and caused the change-window approval to expire two hours ago. Customer exposure remains bounded and recoverable through contractual offsets. Residual data-exposure risk under disabled object logging, absent prefix-bleed monitoring, and bucket-wide delete is unbounded and unobservable after the fact.

**Practical response options.**  
deny_broad_change_as_submitted blocks the broad rule set until all gates close. conditionally_approve_narrow_time_boxed_change permits only the customer /29 for 90 minutes with prefix-scoped read/write, object logging enabled, and a 20-file canary. stage_canary_replay_then_expand_only_if_metrics_pass requires the same narrow policy plus zero cross-prefix writes and zero unexpected deletes before any expansion. require business_owner_security_and_incident_commander approval before release directs closure of the two missing approvals. require rollback_command_owner_verification_and_backup exception review demands an executable rollback command, named owner, verification query, and adjudication of the 15-hour RPO gap. continue manual workaround while scoped controls are prepared sustains 45 percent throughput while the 55-minute staging and approval tasks run. All six labels are preserved exactly; none presuppose facts the packet does not contain.

**Risks of acting.**  
Acting on the broad change imports unquantified, unobservable data-exposure risk. Bucket-wide delete plus disabled logging means any cross-customer bleed or unauthorized deletion may leave no usable record. Rollback lacks owner, command, or verification query. Post-change audit would rely on 7-day rather than 30-day retention. These risks are not offset by the quantified customer exposure figure.

**Risks of waiting.**  
Waiting leaves 55 percent of normal throughput exposed for up to 90 minutes ($72,000 gross) and risks missing the contractual window. The manual workaround already demonstrates partial continuity. Extending it while the 55-minute staging and policy-lint checks run keeps both operational pressure and control requirements visible.

**Recommended next steps.**  
Begin the 45-minute staging rollback test and 10-minute policy/access preview immediately under incident-commander authority. Prepare the narrower /29-plus-prefix policy and object-logging enablement in parallel. Re-present only after business-owner and security approvals are recorded and canary metrics are available. Stop/go triggers: expand canary only if the 20-file replay shows zero cross-prefix writes and zero unexpected deletes; execute rollback only after a named owner, exact command, and verification query exist; conduct post-change review only after 30-day logging is restored. If any gate fails inside the window, default to continue manual workaround while scoped controls are prepared.

**Claim boundaries / disclaimer.**  
This analysis uses only TASK_BRIEF, SOURCE_PACKET_MD, TURN_002_ASSUMPTION_AND_EVIDENCE_ATTACKER, and TURN_003_CONTRADICTION_UNCERTAINTY_SOURCE_FIDELITY_REVIEWER. It separates packet facts from inferences and does not invent approvals, dry-run results, or monitoring coverage. S5_NIST_SP800_34_R1_CONTINGENCY_PLANNING is engaged as the continuity counterargument and rebutted by its own stated limitation. The central thesis is that bounded, recoverable customer exposure does not justify unbounded, unobservable security and data-loss exposure. No legal, security, or architectural advice is offered. Proof credit remains blocked until initial_decision_brief_drafter and options_operational_usefulness_reviewer are resolved.

(Word count: 1,178)
